title: Staleness/refresh + model-version drift process
context: Delivered model cards record the model version and retrieval date at the time of authoring. Models are updated over time; without a process to detect and handle version drift, cards become stale and may misrepresent a model's current state. The schema validator can flag spec drift; this task defines the human process for staleness detection, card refresh, and steward handoff. Depends on validator-006.
objective: Design and publish the staleness/refresh and model-version drift process: defining how drift is detected, when a card becomes a maintenance task, and who owns the ongoing host/maintainer liaison to keep cards current.
acceptanceCriteria: Process document defines the staleness threshold: what constitutes a material model update (new weights version, new eval results, license change, dataset update) that triggers a card refresh task.

Process specifies how drift is detected: the schema validator's specVersions check for tooling drift; periodic review cadence (e.g. quarterly) for model-version drift; steward responsibility for monitoring host/maintainer channels.
Process defines who creates the refresh task: the steward creates a maintenance-type task when drift is detected, routing it to the appropriate reviewer(s).
A named steward (or acting placeholder) is recorded as responsible for ongoing host/maintainer liaison and drift monitoring.
Process document is committed to the project repo and cross-referenced from the canonical schema.
At least one concrete example of a drift scenario and its resolution path is included in the document.
resources: PLAN.md — Sustainability & maintenance; Risks (Model/data versions change)
output: A committed staleness/refresh and model-version drift process document, with a named steward or acting placeholder, committed to the project repo.
